[{"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

I reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.

Once Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.

This should give you the approval chain you need before filing.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T19:48:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "ad391950add941e1bdd66f3754e64a55", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "nia.okafor@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Mon 3/2 onboarding: assign owners and flag document blockers", "content": "Hi George,

Following up on Monday's kickoff, I agree with the split between banking items and general support documents. On the bookkeeping side, I am blocked until I receive the prior balance sheet support, chart of accounts history, and any sales tax or payroll registrations. Once those are in hand, I can finish my review and confirm what is ready to go out versus what still needs client follow-up.

Best,
Nia", "cc": [], "parent_id": "dc9fe1ec878c41a58dc73ba627ad9003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-02T16:01:29Z", "is_read": true}]

content.text: Key findings: there's a recurring "Open May 2026 sales tax cycle" reminder (NY/NJ/CT due June 22, PA due June 25), and the note says to keep PA on the "post-economic-nexus rate used beginning with the April cycle." Let me pull the April thread details, the PA nexus situation, and where April's packet lives.

structuredContent.result:
[{"email_id": "email_scen_045_tax_regulatory_0006", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["priya.singh@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Approved: April sales tax packet and filing submission", "content": "Hi Priya,

All four filings are approved. I'm pleased to see the PA threshold issue was handled cleanly on the first post-crossing return.

Please post the reclassification JE today so it lands in brookfield_FP-2026-04 before lock. Also submit all four returns by the May 20 deadline.

Thanks,
Ming", "cc": ["sofia.halabi@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_045_tax_regulatory_0005", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-04T15:08:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_045_tax_regulatory_0005", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "sofia.halabi@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "April sales tax packet reviewed and ready for sign-off", "content": "Hi Ming,

I reviewed the April Brookfield sales tax packet and it is ready for your sign-off.

Summary by jurisdiction:
New York: $12,400
New Jersey: $6,850
Connecticut: $4,200
Pennsylvania: $3,950

PA is the first return filed at the post-economic-nexus rate following the March threshold crossing, and the return has been reviewed against the updated rate treatment.

Total April sales tax liability is $27,400. Priya has the reclass JE drafted to move the liability from 240000 to 230000 before the April period locks tomorrow.

The reviewed packet is in Records Vault for your sign-off.

Thanks,
Sofia", "cc": ["priya.singh@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-04T13:24:00Z", "is_read": true}]

import json,re
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_78089900-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781602901977.txt'))
obj=json.loads(d['result'])
jes=obj.get('journal_entries') or obj.get('entries') or obj

for je in jes:
    desc=(je.get('description') or '')
    if re.search('sales tax|sales-tax|tax payable|nexus|230000|240000', desc+json.dumps(je.get('lines',[])), re.I):
        print('---', je.get('id'), je.get('status'), '|', desc)
"

content.text: No May prep cut exists in any system, and PA's treatment is established (April was the first post-nexus return at 6%, approved cleanly by Ming). I now have what I need. Let me mark progress and generate the packet content (base64) for the Records Vault upload.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/may_packet.md << 'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS  -  OWN BOOKS
May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet  (STAGED - pending partner approval)

Entity: brookfield        Period: brookfield_FP-2026-05 (May 2026)
Prepared by: Hannah Grant (Corporate Tax Senior)        Date staged: 2026-06-16
Status: STAGED for approval - NOT FILED. Approval authority: Ming Chang.
Filed for trail next to: "Brookfield April 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (doc_1af8dfb919a9444d).

------------------------------------------------------------------
1. WHY THIS IS LATE / WHAT THIS DOES
------------------------------------------------------------------
The standing monthly tickler "Open May 2026 sales tax filing cycle" (due 2026-06-01)
was not actioned, so the May cycle had not been opened and no May work had started.
This packet opens and stages the cycle. The returns are not yet at risk:
  - NY, NJ, CT returns due 2026-06-22
  - PA return due 2026-06-25

------------------------------------------------------------------
2. FILING STATES & STAGED LIABILITY (firm's own taxable services)
------------------------------------------------------------------
  State | Basis                                                  | May 2026 (staged)
  NY    | Taxable consulting/advisory bookings (see note 3)      | $12,400
  NJ    | Trailing-average run-rate                              |  $6,850
  CT    | Trailing-average run-rate                              |  $4,200
  PA    | 6% on PA taxable services - post-economic-nexus (note 4)| $3,950
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  TOTAL STAGED                                                    | $27,400

------------------------------------------------------------------
3. METHODOLOGY & BASIS
------------------------------------------------------------------
- Consistent with the April 2026 cycle (reviewed by Sofia Halabi; approved by Ming
  Chang 2026-05-04). April per-state was NY $12,400 / NJ $6,850 / CT $4,200 /
  PA $3,950 = $27,400.
- NJ and CT carried at the trailing-average run-rate (April was itself described as
  at the trailing 3-month average for these states).
- NY: April's figure carried a partial-month uplift (+18% MoM) from two advisory
  engagements that went live mid-April. In May those run a FULL month. GL Advisory &
  Consulting (403000) recognized rose from $13,413 (Apr) to $26,175 (May),
  corroborating sustained/higher NY taxable activity. NY is therefore the line most
  likely to move UP and must be trued to the final May NY taxable-bookings cut
  before filing. It is staged here at the April run-rate as a floor.
- OPEN PREP INPUT: the per-state May taxable-services cut from brookfield_FP-2026-05
  should be finalized and tied out before submission. The figures above are the
  staged run-rate pending that tie-out; no May sales-tax accrual has been posted yet.

------------------------------------------------------------------
4. PENNSYLVANIA  -  EXPLICIT FLAG (do not assume through)
------------------------------------------------------------------
- PA is newly registered. Brookfield crossed the PA $100K economic-nexus threshold in
  March 2026; the April return was the FIRST post-crossing filing, prepared at 6% on
  PA taxable services. Ming confirmed April's PA treatment was "handled cleanly on the
  first post-crossing return."
- May is carried IDENTICALLY and intentionally: 6% on PA taxable services, monthly
  frequency, May PA return due 2026-06-25. PA has NOT been moved off this basis.
- Flagged rather than assumed: PA is only one cycle old, so the treatment is newly
  established and unseasoned. Two points to CONFIRM at sign-off rather than assume:
    (a) PA taxable-services classification & rate - state 6% applied; no Philadelphia
        (+2%) / Allegheny (+1%) local component is being applied (confirm the firm has
        no local PA situs requiring it);
    (b) the assigned PA filing frequency - treated as monthly, consistent with April.
  If either is unsettled, resolve before filing.

------------------------------------------------------------------
5. RECLASS / JOURNAL ENTRY
------------------------------------------------------------------
- Per the April convention, accrue the May liability and reclass from 240000 to 230000.
  The JE is to be drafted on the same basis and posted into brookfield_FP-2026-05 prior
  to lock. NOT posted as part of staging.

------------------------------------------------------------------
6. ROUTING / SIGN-OFF
------------------------------------------------------------------
- Routed to Ming Chang for approval on 2026-06-16. No return is to be filed until Ming
  approves. Sofia Halabi (April reviewer) copied for continuity.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/may_packet.md > /tmp/may_packet.b64
echo "bytes: $(wc -c < /tmp/may_packet.md)"
echo "b64len: $(wc -c < /tmp/may_packet.b64)"
head -c 120 /tmp/may_packet.b64; echo

input.subject: May 2026 sales tax packet — staged for your approval (not yet filed)
input.content:
Hi Ming,

The May Brookfield sales tax cycle is now opened and staged for your sign-off. Flagging up front: the standing kickoff tickler for May was missed, so this is starting later than usual — but we're not at risk. NY/NJ/CT returns are due June 22 and PA June 25, and nothing will be filed until you approve.

Staged liability by jurisdiction (firm's own books, brookfield_FP-2026-05):
- New York: $12,400
- New Jersey: $6,850
- Connecticut: $4,200
- Pennsylvania: $3,950
- Total staged: $27,400

Basis is consistent with the April cycle you approved on May 4. NJ/CT are carried at the trailing-average run-rate. NY is staged at the April run-rate as a floor — note that the two advisory engagements that drove April's +18% partial-month ran a full month in May (GL Advisory & Consulting recognized roughly doubled MoM), so NY is the line most likely to move up and should be trued to the final May NY taxable-bookings cut before filing. The per-state May taxable-services cut from FP-2026-05 is the remaining prep input to tie out; no sales-tax accrual has been posted yet.

Pennsylvania — flagging rather than assuming it through, per the request to handle PA carefully:
- PA is carried IDENTICALLY to April: 6% on PA taxable services on the post-economic-nexus schedule (threshold crossed March; April was the first post-crossing return). I have not moved PA off that basis.
- Because PA is only one cycle old, two items I'd like you to confirm at sign-off rather than have me assume: (a) the PA taxable-services classification/rate — state 6% only, no Philadelphia (+2%)/Allegheny (+1%) local component applied; and (b) the assigned monthly filing frequency. If either is unsettled we should resolve before filing.

Full write-up is in Records Vault next to April's: "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (doc_cd526e7106a0), filed alongside the April packet (doc_1af8dfb919a9444d).

Per the April convention, the accrual/reclass JE (240000 -> 230000) will be drafted on the same basis and posted into FP-2026-05 once you've approved.

content.text:
The May sales-tax cycle is now open, staged, and routed for approval. Here's where things landed.

## What I found
- The **May kickoff tickler** ("Open May 2026 sales tax filing cycle," due June 1) was indeed never actioned, and **no May work had started** — no sales-tax accrual is posted in `brookfield_FP-2026-05`. You're not in crisis: NY/NJ/CT are due **June 22**, PA **June 25**.
- **April's packet** lives in Records Vault: *"Brookfield April 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet"* (`doc_1af8dfb919a9444d`, tax_return / restricted / IRS_TAX_7Y). April's approved figures (from Sofia's review + Ming's approval): **NY $12,400 / NJ $6,850 / CT $4,200 / PA $3,950 = $27,400**.

## Pennsylvania — handled deliberately, and flagged
PA crossed the **$100K economic-nexus threshold in March**; April was the **first** post-crossing return at **6% on taxable services**, which Ming confirmed was "handled cleanly." I carried May **identically — 6%, post-nexus, monthly** — and did **not** move it off that basis. Because PA is only one cycle old, I flagged (rather than assumed) two confirmation items for Ming's sign-off: (a) the 6% rate with **no Philadelphia/Allegheny local component**, and (b) the **monthly filing frequency**.

## May staged liability
NY $12,400 · NJ $6,850 · CT $4,200 · PA $3,950 — **$27,400 total**. NJ/CT/PA are at the trailing run-rate; NY is staged at the April floor with a note that it should be **trued to the final May NY taxable-bookings cut** before filing (the two mid-April advisory engagements ran a full month in May — GL Advisory & Consulting roughly doubled MoM). One caveat worth your eye: the firm's per-state *taxable-services cut* isn't reconstructable from the GL alone (the GL carries firm-wide cumulative revenue), so the precise per-state tie-out from `FP-2026-05` is the remaining prep input before filing.

## Done
1. **Staged the write-up in Records Vault** next to April's: `doc_cd526e7106a0` (same kind/classification/retention).
2. **Routed to Ming for approval** (cc Sofia) — explicitly *not yet filed*; the accrual/reclass JE (240000→230000) will be drafted after sign-off.
3. **Posted a note** to #tax-prep-and-filings that May is moving.
4. **Set your reminder** (due **June 18**) covering both deadlines, the PA confirmation items, and the NY true-up.

Nothing gets filed until Ming approves — the sign-off stays his.
